title: Validation severity vocabulary
description: Formal shape of validation-severity.yaml — the three severity classes (hard-reject, bypassable-with-audit, warning) the methodology tags every validation rule with, plus the bypass / audit-comment / abort semantics each class implies. Other schemas reference entries here via the typed token `[validation-severity:<id>]` (COR-019). Companion per COR-018 / the .pkit/schemas/ area conventions.

severities.description: Severity classes. Keys are kebab-case ids; values match $defs.entry.

summary.description: One-line description of when this severity applies.

behaviour.description: What the agent does when a rule of this severity is violated.

bypassable.description: Whether the user may override the agent's refusal. Mutually informative with `aborts_operation`.

aborts_operation.description: Whether the agent halts the operation on violation. `true` for hard-reject and bypassable-with-audit (unless the user bypasses); `false` for warning.

audit_comment_template.description: When `bypassable: true`, the comment format the agent posts on the affected issue/PR before the mutation runs. Placeholders use `<name>` style. Omit when `bypassable: false`.

override_syntax.description: User-facing override syntax (e.g., `--bypass "<reason>"`). Present only when `bypassable: true`.

examples.description: Representative rules tagged with this severity, sourced from the upstream methodology. Illustrative, not exhaustive.

notes.description: Free-form notes that don't fit other fields (rationale fragments, edge cases). Optional.

entry.description: Per-severity shape. Each entry names the class and pins the agent's behaviour for it.

severity_ref.description: Narrowed cross-schema reference token (COR-019) pointing at an entry in `validation-severity.yaml`. Consuming schemas $ref this $def when a field's value identifies a severity class.
